title: Savory Steamed Russian Buckwheat Pilaf
description: A creamy, cozy Russian dish featuring yuzu, papillote, and a hint of nuttiness from buckwheat. Perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Rinse the buckwheat groats in a fine mesh sieve and drain well.
In a large saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the buckwheat groats and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add the yuzu juice, thyme, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine.
Add the water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover with a papillote, and simmer for 20 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it sit, covered, for 5 minutes.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve hot.
